title: zanthoxylum fagara subsp. culantrilo
intro: Zanthoxylum fagara subsp. culantrilo, also known as Culantro or Mexican coriander, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a member of the Rutaceae family and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal compounds. The health benefits of Zanthoxylum fagara subsp. culantrilo include the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, are often used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
